rationale: B is closer to the actual Kafka Connect 4.2.0 validation surface: `Struct`, `ConnectSchema`, `SchemaBuilderTest`, and `StructTest` are the right anchors. A’s versioned docs are good, but its test guidance is mis-anchored to unrelated integration tests and incorrectly implies no Struct-specific test exists, which reduces trust.

A: Uses unrelated integration tests (`ConnectorValidationIntegrationTest`, `ExactlyOnceSourceIntegrationTest`) instead of the real neighboring unit tests for Struct validation.
Claims no Struct-specific test was surfaced, but `connect/api/src/test/java/org/apache/kafka/connect/data/StructTest.java` exists.
Misses the most relevant adjacent tests: `ConnectSchemaTest` and `SchemaBuilderTest`.
B: Does not give exact 4.2.0 doc URLs, only stable class-name anchors.
Could mention `ValuesTest` as another adjacent validation test because Struct validation flows through Connect data validation helpers.
